steps.title: Apply distance = speed times time
steps.content: Walking distance: $2 \text{ mph} \times w \text{ hours} = 2w$ miles. Running distance: $6 \text{ mph} \times r \text{ hours} = 6r$ miles.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $2w + 6r = 10$: Matches the speed $\times$ time sum equal to total miles. Correct.
  • B) $\frac{1}{2}w + \frac{1}{6}r = 10$: Uses reciprocals of speeds (units of hours per mile), giving hours, not miles. Eliminated.
  • C) $2w + 6r = 80$: Right left side, but total miles is $10$, not $80$. Eliminated.
  • D) $\frac{1}{2}w + \frac{1}{6}r = 80$: Wrong coefficients and wrong total. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
